parsed_review.summary: CP00-253 (Risk B, 40 units, RP07.P05.M02.S15-RP07.P05.M04.S12) is a descriptor-only search-core fixture slice pack that closes the RP07.P05.M02 type/shape tail and builds the M03 primary + M04 secondary fixture cycles. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and NOT overclaimed: both manifest.json and command-evidence.json record production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout (production_ready:true is correctly deferred to post-review construction-inspection, as in CP00-252). Pack identity is consistent across the live closeout-pack-plan.json (line 412, deviation_from_plan:false, within_default_risk_range:true), SEARCH_CORE_CP253_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:325), the baseline, the manifest, and contracts/search-core-contract.json current_pack — all agree on risk B, 40 units, range, upstream CP00-252, next CP00-254 / RP07.P05.M04.S13, and production_ready_flag search_core_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified. The pack chain is contiguous (CP252 ends S14 and points next->CP253/S15; CP253 spans S15-S12 and points next->CP254/S13). All distributions were independently re-counted from included_units and sum to 40: deliverable (test6/hermes_evidence2/implementation13/fixture13/claude_review2/security_audit4), micro-phase (M02:6,M03:22,M04:12), and micro-title (Type And Shape 6 / Primary Implementation 22 / Secondary Workflow 12), matching the prompt baseline. All 7 required public exports exist (createSearchCoreCp253FixtureSliceCaseSet/Descriptor in service.js; validateSearchCoreCp253Coverage/FixtureSliceDescriptor, createSearchCoreCp253HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff in validators.js) and resolve via index.js export * (so index.js being unmodified is expected). Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries hold: freezeCp253Result zeroes ~30 runtime/write/db/object-storage/audit/AI/Hermes-receipt/leak flags and sets synthetic_only/no_real_data/customer_safe_errors_only; every fixture row sets descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; row extras enforce stable_id_check.id_drift_detected:false, replay_command.executes_command_runtime:false, cross_tenant_access_allowed:false, dispatches_ai_runtime:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, claude_final_approval_claimed:false, real_client_data_loaded:false, and security_trimming unauthorized_data_omitted:true; no writeFileSync/fetch/http/process.env/child_process/DB calls and no PII/secret patterns appear in the search source. Drift prevention is enforced: scripts/validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs and test/model.test.js deep-equal contract.fixture_slice_descriptor, cp253_requirements, and cp253_no_write_attestation against the frozen registry constants, and assert historical_pack_bindings tail == CP00-253. The Claude read-only / not-final-approver boundary is preserved (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, read_only:true, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet metadata (receipt_shape descriptor_only_search_fixture_slice_no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening checks pass: readiness auth-preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1432-byte stdout, valid JSON envelope), prompt is 2253 bytes with no embedded full diff/source, summary-only review is prohibited and not used, forbidden tools absent, and there is no prior raw-output.json/review-receipt.json for CP00-253 (this is the only review run; invalid_attempts_preserved is empty), so no malformed/auth-failed/0-byte/tool-call-shaped review evidence requires rejection. The absence of claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-253/ is expected because those are downstream products of this review. CAVEAT: under the read-only mandate I could not independently execute the listed validation commands (npm test, npm run validate/build, rp07:search-core:validate, etc.); this review verifies the source/artifact consistency that supports the recorded exit_code:0 evidence. Two non-blocking P3 observations are noted below (a harness authority-boundary wording ambiguity carried over from CP00-252, and an unrelated out-of-scope LDIP review artifact present in the working tree). Nothing blocks pack or goal closeout; final enterprise-trust closure remains a human responsibility outside this receipt.

findings.title: Harness authority_boundary token enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true reads as ambiguous against human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim:true
findings.details: The harness-provided authority_boundary sets enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true (prompt.txt line 44; identical block in review-baseline.json and review-request inputs) while the adjacent field human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim:true (line 43) and review-packet.md ('Human/final approval and enterprise trust claim: closed outside this receipt') indicate the claim is deferred to human approval, i.e. resolved OUTSIDE this automated receipt rather than satisfied by it. Read in isolation, the boolean enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true could be misread as the enterprise-trust claim already being satisfied. The pack implementation itself is unambiguous and correct: descriptor.authority_boundary uses claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true (service.js:2542-2547), and SEARCH_CORE_CP253_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. This is a wording/clarity nit in the review-harness boundary token, not a defect in the pack code, and it does not weaken any gate. Same observation was raised on CP00-252.
findings.recommendation: For future review packets, rename or annotate the token (e.g. enterprise_trust_claim_closed_outside_this_receipt) so the deferred-to-human semantics are unambiguous and cannot be read as a satisfied enterprise-trust claim. No change required to CP00-253 implementation.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; non-blocking. Surfaced for the human final approver. Pack closeout may proceed; enterprise-trust closure remains gated on human approval outside this receipt.

findings.title: Unrelated out-of-scope LDIP review artifact present in working tree; confirmed not CP00-253 review evidence
findings.details: The working tree contains an untracked review artifact for a DIFFERENT track (review_code C-LDIP-01, overall_verdict BLOCK), stored as a raw claude CLI result envelope ({"type":"result",...}) with the review JSON embedded in the .result string. changed-file-scope.json correctly classifies it under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and it is absent from CP00-253 implementation_refs/evidence_refs, so it is NOT and must not be treated as CP00-253 review evidence (CP00-253 invalid_attempts_preserved is empty). Two items inside that file are worth surfacing to the human approver as repo hygiene, both OUTSIDE CP00-253 scope: (a) it asserts the untracked docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md embeds a <system-reminder>...</system-reminder> block within document content, which is a prompt-injection vector against future automated readers; (b) it reports anomalous non-git text appearing in a git status tool result during that other review. Neither file is part of CP00-253 and I did not rely on either; per the prompt I did not infer these unrelated dirty files into this pack.
findings.recommendation: Keep these artifacts out of CP00-253 closeout (already done by changed-file-scope). Separately, for the LDIP track, strip the embedded <system-reminder> from new-session-handoff.md and store review results as schema-validated files rather than raw CLI envelopes. No change required to CP00-253.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; non-blocking and out-of-scope for CP00-253. Flagged for human awareness of repo hygiene / injection vector in unrelated untracked files; does not affect CP00-253 pack or goal closeout.
